label: near_miss

case_target.description: 当前 case 的主控制对象是 _resolve_safe_path()，它负责规范化用户路径并限制在基目录内。

should_not_report.reason: The path is fully validated by _resolve_safe_path() before open() is called. normpath normalizes separators, isabs() rejects absolute paths, and realpath() + prefix check is the true security boundary that resolves symlinks and all remaining '..' sequences.
should_not_report.false_positive_risk: A system that matches the pattern 'request.args → os.path.join → open()' without tracing into _resolve_safe_path() will incorrectly flag open() at line 32 as a vulnerable sink.

controls.description: os.path.normpath normalizes redundant separators and resolves non-leading '..' components (e.g. a/b/../c → a/c), but preserves leading '..' in relative paths (../../etc/passwd stays as-is). It is not the security boundary; its role is to reduce noise before realpath.
Explicit rejection of absolute paths after normalization, blocking '/etc/passwd'-style inputs.
os.path.realpath resolves symlinks and any remaining '..' components to a canonical absolute path.
Prefix check ensures the resolved path is strictly inside BASE_DIR. os.sep suffix prevents partial-directory name bypass (e.g. /var/www/files-evil).

notes: The false-positive trigger is intentional: the surface pattern (request param → join → open) is identical to PT-PY-FILE-001. Correct behavior requires inter-procedural analysis of _resolve_safe_path.
